skill.name: gh-address-comments
skill.description: Guide assistants through resolving GitHub pull request comments with the GitHub CLI, authentication checks, user triage, and selected fixes.
skill.summary: Resolve GitHub pull request comments with gh CLI guidance and user-approved fixes.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: The static weak-cryptography findings at SKILL.md lines 3 and 5 are false positives; those lines only contain descriptive metadata. The external-command findings are valid because the skill instructs use of gh, elevated network permissions, and broad auth scopes. No evidence of malware, credential exfiltration, or prompt injection was found in the reviewed file.

medium_findings.title: GitHub CLI Commands Require Elevated Network Access
medium_findings.description: The skill directs assistants to run gh commands with elevated network access and to retry auth commands with elevated sandbox permissions. This is legitimate for GitHub PR work, but it increases exposure if repository credentials or command context are mishandled.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly instructs use of gh commands and elevated network permissions. The purpose is legitimate, but the permission elevation creates a real operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Broad GitHub Authentication Scope Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill tells users to include workflow and repo scopes for gh authentication. These scopes can grant broad repository and workflow access, so users should confirm the scope is necessary for the target repository.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scope guidance is explicit and relevant to privileged GitHub operations. The file does not show malicious use, so this remains a medium-risk concern.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at metadata lines, but those lines only describe the skill and its short description. No cryptographic algorithm or cryptographic operation is present there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: There is no evidence of weak cryptography in the cited lines. This appears to be a pattern-based false positive rather than a security issue.

dangerous_patterns.title: External GitHub CLI Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs assistants to use gh commands for PR comment handling. External command execution is expected for this workflow but should run only with user-approved repository context.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly mention gh command use. The risk comes from external command and network access, not from hidden code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Elevated Permission Request
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references sandbox_permissions=require_escalated for gh auth status when sandboxing blocks the command. This can be acceptable, but users should review the exact command before approval.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The permission request is explicit in the skill text. It is not framed as a system override, but it does request a higher execution privilege.

content.value_statement: Review comments can be hard to triage across GitHub threads and local code changes. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through authenticated gh CLI review handling.
actual_capabilities: Guides assistants to find the open pull request for the current branch with GitHub CLI.
Requires GitHub CLI authentication checks before comment work begins.
Directs assistants to collect PR review threads and issue comments for user triage.
Structures comments into numbered items with short fix summaries.
Tells assistants to apply fixes only after the user selects comments.
Prompts re-authentication when GitHub CLI auth or rate limits interrupt work.
limitations: Depends on an installed and authenticated GitHub CLI.
References scripts/fetch_comments.py, but this audited package only contains SKILL.md.
Requires network access to GitHub and repository permissions.
Does not provide its own parser or code modification implementation.

use_cases.title: Triage Review Threads
use_cases.description: Collect open pull request comments, summarize each thread, and decide which items need changes first.

use_cases.title: Prepare Focused Fixes
use_cases.description: Select specific reviewer comments and guide an assistant to apply only those requested fixes.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Repository Review Work
use_cases.description: Create a clear list of unresolved comments before assigning fixes across a team.

prompt_templates.title: List PR Comments
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for understanding review feedback.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the gh-address-comments skill to find my current branch pull request and list comments that need attention.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize Review Threads
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow for planning fixes before editing files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gh-address-comments to summarize each unresolved PR review thread with the likely code area and fix effort.

prompt_templates.title: Address Selected Items
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for controlled implementation after triage.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gh-address-comments to number the review comments, ask me which items to fix, then address only my selected items.

prompt_templates.title: Recover From Auth Issues
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow when GitHub CLI access fails during review work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gh-address-comments to check gh authentication, explain any missing access, and continue PR comment handling after I approve re-authentication.

output_examples.input: Find comments on my current pull request.
output: A numbered list of review threads needing attention.
A short summary of the requested change for each thread.
A question asking which numbered items the user wants fixed.
A focused plan for the selected comments.
Applied changes limited to the chosen review items.
A concise summary of what changed and what remains.
A clear explanation that gh authentication is required.
A prompt asking the user to authenticate before retrying.
A retry of the PR comment workflow after access is restored.

best_practices: Review the exact gh command before approving elevated network access.
Select specific comment numbers before allowing code changes.
Run tests or targeted checks after applying reviewer-requested fixes.
anti_patterns: Do not approve broad GitHub scopes without confirming repository needs.
Do not apply every review comment without user selection.
Do not rely on this skill when scripts/fetch_comments.py is unavailable.

faq.question: Does this skill require GitHub CLI?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow depends on gh commands and an authenticated GitHub session.

faq.question: Can it work without network access?
faq.answer: No. It needs GitHub network access to inspect pull request comments and authentication status.

faq.question: Does it automatically fix every comment?
faq.answer: No. It instructs the assistant to ask which numbered comments should be addressed first.

faq.question: Does the package include comment fetching code?
faq.answer: No. The audited package only contains SKILL.md and references scripts/fetch_comments.py.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill requests external gh commands, elevated network access, and broad GitHub auth scopes.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
